children.text: identify agency compliance activities to meet the requirements of section 543 of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act (

children.tail: ) and any other matters relevant to implementing the goals of such Act; and

children.text: determine if the agency has the internal accounting mechanisms necessary to assess the accuracy and reliability of energy consumption and energy cost figures required under such section.
President’s Council on Integrity and Efficiency report to Congress

children.tail: , the President’s Council on Integrity and Efficiency shall submit a report to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources and the Committee on Governmental Affairs of the Senate, the Committee on Energy and Commerce, the Committee on Government Operations, and the Committee on Public Works and Transportation of the House of Representatives, on the review conducted by the Inspector General of each agency under this section.
is encouraged to conduct periodic reviews of agency compliance with part 3 of title V of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act [
et seq.], the provisions of this subtitle,
See References in Text note below.
and other laws relating to energy consumption. Such reviews shall not be inconsistent with the performance of the required duties of the Inspector General’s office.

children.text: The National Energy Conservation Policy Act, referred to in subsecs. (a)(1) and (c), is

children.text: This subtitle, referred to in subsec. (c), is subtitle F (§§ 151–168) of title I of

children.text: Section was enacted as part of the Energy Policy Act of 1992, and not as part of the National Energy Conservation Policy Act which comprises this chapter.
